# LumiaForge AI Engineering Standards

An auditable engineering operating model for agent-assisted software work: a machine-readable requirement registry, portable agent role conventions, governance templates, and deterministic scripts — not a prompt library or a RAG platform.

<!-- Generated by scripts/llms-txt-generator.py from registry/tr-registry.yaml, agents/, templates/, and scripts/. Do not edit by hand — regenerate instead. -->

## Registry

Machine-readable source: `registry/tr-registry.yaml`.

### Secrets Management

- **TR-SEC-001** — No hardcoded secrets: All secrets must be stored in a vault. Never hardcoded in configuration files, source code, comments, or test fixtures.
- **TR-SEC-008** — Local credential files are permission-restricted and secret-scanned: Files holding secrets on a development or production host (.env files, key files, token caches) must be readable by the owning user only (mode 600). While secrets live in local files rather than a vault (TR-SEC-001's target state), a secret-scanning check must run before commit, not only at public-release time. Counters MITRE ATT&CK T1552.001 (Unsecured Credentials: Credentials in Files).

### Data Privacy

- **TR-SEC-002** — PII encrypted at rest: Personally identifiable information must be encrypted at rest when stored by an application.

### Agents

- **TR-SEC-003** — PII routed to local LLM only: For privacy-focused use cases, local LLMs must be used. Cloud-backed agents must not access private notes, secrets, private repos, or unrestricted home directories.

### Prompt Injection

- **TR-SEC-005** — Externally retrieved content treated as untrusted: Retrieved content must not authorize tool calls or override system-level reasoning. When retrieved content may contain PII, TR-SEC-003 routing takes precedence.

### CI/CD Supply Chain

- **TR-SEC-009** — CI pipelines run least-privilege and fully pinned: Every CI workflow declares an explicit least-privilege permissions block (default contents: read; broader scopes granted per-job with a comment stating why). Third-party CI actions are pinned to a full commit SHA, not a mutable tag, with the human-readable version in a trailing comment. Runtime dependencies past POC tier are pinned to exact versions so the audited dependency set is the set that runs. Counters MITRE ATT&CK T1195 (Supply Chain Compromise) and limits the blast radius of a compromised workflow token.

### Agent Security

- **TR-SEC-010** — Agent tool permission grants are a security boundary (least agency): Permission allowlists for coding agents (e.g. tool-grant or command-allowlist config) are security boundaries, not conveniences: a prompt-injected agent session (TR-SEC-005) can invoke any allowlisted command without human review. Wildcard grants for arbitrary file writes, package installation, unrestricted exec, or unrestricted network access must not be allowlisted; grant the specific command needed instead. This is OWASP's least-agency principle (least privilege extended to what an agent tool can do, how often, and where) applied to agent tooling. Review all agent permission grants at each governance review (TR-GOV-006). Counters MITRE ATT&CK T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) and T1195.002 executed through trusted developer tooling.

### Testing

- **TR-TEST-001** — TDD required for core logic: Tests are written before or alongside core logic. Scaffolding may come first, but business logic should not be considered complete without tests.
- **TR-TEST-002** — Coverage targets by deployment tier: POC projects may use targeted coverage. Local Production targets at least 70 percent coverage. Cloud Production targets at least 90 percent coverage.
- **TR-TEST-004** — LLM regression benchmark on model switch: When switching or upgrading a runtime LLM, run a standard task set against the old and new models and record cost, latency, correctness, and schema validation deltas. See templates/llm-regression-benchmark.md.
- **TR-TEST-005** — LLM eval files co-located with agent modules: Each agent module that makes LLM calls should have a co-located tests/evals/test_<agent>_eval.py file with golden cases, a score function, and an LLM_EVAL=true guard so evals do not run in the standard unit suite. See templates/llm-eval.md.
- **TR-TEST-006** — Post-write state change verification for agent actions: Agent actions that produce external side effects, such as writing files, updating records, sending messages, or calling tools, should be followed by a deterministic verification step that confirms the observable state changed as expected.

### Architecture Decision Records

- **TR-ADR-001** — ADR required for significant design decisions: An ADR must be created for each significant design decision including agent framework choice and trigger classification (TR-AGT-004).
- **TR-GOV-005** — AI system impact assessment: ADRs that change AI behavior toward people require templates/ai-impact-assessment.md.

### Agent Spawning

- **TR-AGT-003** — Agentic node loop contracts: Every agent pipeline node declares input schema, output schema, exit condition, and resource budget before implementation. Nodes exposed as MCP tools additionally declare four tool annotation hints (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, idempotentHint, openWorldHint) describing blast radius — required only for MCP-exposed nodes.
- **TR-AGT-002** — Deterministic operations use scripts not agents: Operations that require repeatable results should be implemented in scripts, code, queries, tests, or validators. Agents may call those deterministic tools, but should not replace them.
- **TR-AGT-004** — Agent trigger type classified at design time: Each agent invocation is classified as event-driven, scheduled, or user-initiated. Undocumented triggers are non-compliant unmanaged side effects.
- **TR-AGT-005** — Behavioral mode declaration: A behavioral mode is a named, trigger-activated instruction set that changes how an agent approaches a task, orthogonal to process-intensity gate strictness. Each mode declares a trigger, activated behavior, exit condition, and precedence (safety and process-intensity rules always win on conflict).

### Development Conventions

- **TR-GOV-001** — Config/model-string consistency check: scripts/check-config-consistency.py detects retired model strings and drift within an app. Run before merge; CI integration recommended.
- **TR-GOV-002** — Deferred-work tagging convention: Tag deferred work as LUMIA-DEBT or POC-EXCEPTION. scripts/debt-report.py compiles tags for maturity checklist reconciliation.
- **TR-GOV-003** — Process intensity mode declared per work session: Work sessions may declare a process intensity mode, such as full or poc-fast. Security and agent safety requirements still apply regardless of mode; deferred gates must be recorded as structured debt or POC exceptions.

### App Starter Template

- **TR-GOV-004** — Per-app maturity checklist: Each app maintains docs/maturity-checklist.md mapping governance mechanisms to ISO 42001-inspired control objectives.

### Documentation

- **TR-GOV-006** — Recurring governance review cadence: Governance reviewed at tier graduations and at minimum every 90 days using templates/governance-review.md.

## Agent Roles

Tool-neutral role specs for developer, reviewer, private researcher, and public researcher agents.

- [agents/developer.md](agents/developer.md) — Developer Agent
- [agents/private-researcher.md](agents/private-researcher.md) — Private Researcher Agent
- [agents/public-researcher.md](agents/public-researcher.md) — Public Researcher Agent
- [agents/reviewer.md](agents/reviewer.md) — Reviewer Agent

## Templates

Governance and eval templates: ADR, impact assessment, maturity checklist, completion checklist, LLM eval.

- [templates/adr.md](templates/adr.md) — ADR-NNNN: [Short Title]
- [templates/ai-impact-assessment.md](templates/ai-impact-assessment.md) — AI Impact Assessment: [Short Title]
- [templates/completion-checklist.md](templates/completion-checklist.md) — Completion Checklist
- [templates/governance-review.md](templates/governance-review.md) — Governance Review — Cycle [N] — [Date]
- [templates/llm-eval.md](templates/llm-eval.md) — LLM Eval Template
- [templates/llm-regression-benchmark.md](templates/llm-regression-benchmark.md) — LLM Regression Benchmark: [old-model] → [new-model]
- [templates/maturity-checklist.md](templates/maturity-checklist.md) — Maturity Checklist: [App Name]
- [templates/threat-model.md](templates/threat-model.md) — Threat Model: [Short Title]

## Scripts

Deterministic reference scripts (stdlib-only).

- [scripts/agent-permission-guard.py](scripts/agent-permission-guard.py) — TR-SEC-010 -- Agent tool permission allowlist guard (least agency).
- [scripts/check-config-consistency.py](scripts/check-config-consistency.py) — TR-GOV-001 — Config/model-string consistency check.
- [scripts/cursor-rules-adapter.py](scripts/cursor-rules-adapter.py) — Cursor rules adapter — generates .cursor/rules/*.mdc from the TR registry.
- [scripts/debt-report.py](scripts/debt-report.py) — TR-GOV-002 — Deferred-work tagging convention report.
- [scripts/llms-txt-generator.py](scripts/llms-txt-generator.py) — llms.txt generator — cross-tool discovery manifest generated from the TR registry.
- [scripts/public-export-check.py](scripts/public-export-check.py) — TR-PUB-002 / TR-PUB-003 — Validate a public standards repo checkout.
